since.window: 0 new scheduler commits at run start (HEAD == anchor 2dd01bc8); driven by 3 user [AUDIT note]s about the Conversations feature. 3 commits landed this run. pytest 486 passed / 3 skipped; rsched-lint green; journal util available (server tz = UTC).

summary: Feature/bugfix run: acted on all three reviewer [AUDIT note]s about conversations, each a separate test-gated commit. (1) F47 conversation state diagram now lights the live reply-cycle state (0.17.0, aa6c6fe138). (2) F48 dual conversation budgets — per-reply turns + a new cumulative max_total_turns cap, both settable in the New conversation view (0.18.0, c46339c2fb). (3) F49 run timestamps normalized to UTC end-to-end, fixing the ~2h UI clock skew and a latent catchup double-fire (0.19.0, 42f559133d). Suite green throughout; restart requested. Carried open: F45/F46 (deferred-answer/subrun-question drainage) and F50 (elapsed_s tz fallback) remain unfixed candidates.

findings.title: Conversation state diagram never showed the current state — FIXED this run
findings.detail: The Conversations tab's 'state' rail rendered the generic routine state graph, which parses the converse workflow's single 'conversation' phase and lights the node named by state/phase.json — but a conversation is free-form and never writes phase.json, so no node ever highlighted (AUDIT note 1). Fixed: /api/conversations/{slug}/stategraph now returns a two-node reply-cycle graph (working <-> waiting for you) with the current node derived from the live run state (conversations.conversation_phase), and views/conversations.js re-lights it from setState on every SSE state event (was feeding the always-empty s.phase). Committed aa6c6fe138 (0.17.0) with endpoint + mapping tests.

findings.title: Conversations lacked a whole-conversation budget separate from per-reply turns — FIXED this run
findings.detail: Conversation budgets were per-reply only (max_turns resets each reply; usage_base carries only for reporting, not enforcement). Added max_total_turns to DEFAULT_BUDGETS (-1=unlimited), enforced in budget_violation/budget_warning against the cumulative ctx.turn (restored across resume windows at loop.py:264), so a conversation can be capped as a whole while each reply keeps its own small window. Both budgets are now settable in the 'New conversation' view and POST /api/conversations accepts max_turns/max_total_turns form fields (AUDIT note 2). Committed c46339c2fb (0.18.0) with engine + endpoint tests.

findings.title: Run timestamps skewed ~2h (UTC server vs Berlin viewer) — FIXED this run
findings.detail: Server runs in UTC (shell: date = UTC +0000, TZ unset); routines/viewer are Europe/Berlin. run_ts() emitted server-local (UTC here) but a bare YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS carries no offset, and consumers disagreed: registry.parse_run_ts stamped the ROUTINE tz (Berlin) and the UI's toDate parsed BROWSER-local — a ~2h skew (AUDIT note 3), and parse_run_ts's mis-stamp could make missed_fire spuriously re-fire a catchup:run_once routine on a UTC host. Fixed to UTC end-to-end: ids.run_ts always UTC, registry.parse_run_ts reads run-ts as UTC, static/util.js toDate parses run-ts as UTC then renders in the VIEWER's local time. Committed 42f559133d (0.19.0) with tests. Residual: F50.

findings.title: elapsed_s fallback in registry.read_run still treats stamps as naive
findings.detail: registry.read_run's pre-elapsed_s fallback (used only for old runs lacking status.elapsed_s) computes elapsed from strptime(started) [now UTC] minus fromisoformat(updated).replace(tzinfo=None). Correct on a UTC host (both naive UTC), but on a non-UTC host the UTC run-ts vs server-local updated would skew this rarely-hit fallback. Left unfixed this run to keep the F49 diff minimal; make both stamps UTC-aware in a follow-up.

findings.title: collect_deferred_answers leaves unmatched inbox answers forever (carried, open)
findings.detail: Carried from prior run. Unmatched deferred-answer inbox files are never cleaned up or dead-lettered; two such orphans were manually removed in the 07-14 run. Candidate scheduler fix with tests: expire/dead-letter answers with no matching pending question. Not acted this run (the three AUDIT notes took priority).
evidence: prior run 20260714-194518 LEDGER (two orphan answer files removed)

findings.title: subrun-filed deferred questions in runs/<ts>/sub/<n>/questions/pending are never surfaced/drained (carried, open)
findings.detail: Carried from prior run. A finished subrun has no next run, so a deferred question it filed under runs/<ts>/sub/<n>/questions/pending/ is neither shown on the Decisions page nor drained. Candidate fix: surface subrun pending questions at the parent/root level. Not acted this run.
evidence: prior run 20260714-194518 LEDGER (orphan subrun pending question removed)

findings.title: Test-fixture hermetic-home isolation gap (carried, benign)
findings.detail: Unchanged. conftest _hermetic_home patches rsched.config.expand but not paths.expand/RSCHED_CONFIG; benign today because tests dodge it with inline keys/monkeypatches. Close in the fixture if more tests trip on it.

findings.title: status.json elapsed_s resets on resume (carried, cosmetic)
findings.detail: Carried. Mildly skews the Stats tab compute-time total on resumed runs (relevant to conversations, which resume per reply). Low value; candidate for a future improve-bugfix lens.

findings.title: newsletter-digest references a missing step file (carried, routine-owned)
findings.detail: Carried. newsletter-digest recipe references steps/update-last-processed.md which does not exist; outside this audit's writable root (routine-improver territory). Report-only.
